disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt-onset AND abrupt-offset ('like a switch flipped'), perfectly regular, very rapid palpitations starting at rest
trigger_question_examples: How did the racing start and stop - suddenly or gradually?
Was the racing regular or irregular, and how fast did it feel?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started completely out of the blue while I was just sitting there, raced incredibly fast and regularly, then stopped just as suddenly - like a switch flipping off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Presyncope during the episode (nearly passed out, had to lie flat on the floor) with mild chest tightness
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel dizzy or like you might pass out during it?
Any chest pain or breathlessness with the racing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I felt so lightheaded I nearly blacked out and had to lie down on the floor, and my chest felt tight while it was happening.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent self-terminating episodes (three this year) in an otherwise very healthy young person with good exercise tolerance
trigger_question_examples: Has this happened before, and how often?
Are you otherwise fit and well between episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's happened three times this year, each one just stopping on its own. Otherwise I'm really healthy and can exercise fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Episode has fully self-resolved; she feels completely back to normal now and denies actually fainting and denies severe crushing chest pain
trigger_question_examples: How do you feel right now - is the racing completely gone?
Did you actually lose consciousness, or have severe crushing chest pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The racing has completely stopped and I feel normal again now. I didn't actually pass out, and I didn't have severe crushing chest pain - just the tightness during it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Uses energy drinks / pre-workout supplements and has moderate caffeine intake; no regular medications; occasional alcohol
trigger_question_examples: Do you use any caffeine, energy drinks, or pre-workout supplements?
Any regular medications or recreational stimulants?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have a fair bit of caffeine and I sometimes use energy drinks and pre-workout powders. I'm not on any regular medications and only drink alcohol occasionally.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: It's stopped now and I feel fine - is there anything I actually need to do, or can I just forget about it?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should not dismiss it just because it has resolved. Should explain that abrupt-onset/offset regular racing with near-fainting, recurring in a young healthy person, points to a re-entrant fast-heart-rhythm (paroxysmal SVT, possibly from an extra electrical pathway) that needs a priority resting heart tracing (12-lead ECG) to look for a specific pattern, plus a cardiology/electrophysiology review, because confirming that pattern changes how any future episode is treated.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: If it comes back, is there something I can take or do at home to stop it?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should coach a safe vagal manoeuvre (a modified Valsalva - bearing down while lying, then legs raised) as a first self-help attempt for a future episode, and explicitly should NOT recommend self-medicating with any AV-nodal blocking drug at home; should advise that if a future episode does not self-terminate, causes actual fainting, or comes with severe chest pain, she must call 000, and that the priority ECG and specialist review are needed to guide any future drug treatment safely.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may want to write it off as anxiety/panic or a one-off now that it has stopped - tests whether the AI Doctor still recognises probable paroxysmal SVT/pre-excitation and secures a priority ECG and specialist review.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: It always just stops by itself and I feel fine after - maybe it's only my anxiety and I'm overreacting?
